DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-22-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SUTTER PACIFIC MEDICAL FOUNDATION, SUTTER HEALTH, SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT filed on August 19, 2015, is overruled as to the second cause of action for medical negligence (survival action) and the third cause of action for failure to obtain informed consent and sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress. As to the second cause of action, the failure to file an affidavit in compliance with CCP 377.32 is not a basis to sustain a demurrer. "[T]his provision does not require that the affidavit be filed as a condition precedent to commencing or continuing the action. However, failure to file the affidavit could possibly subject the action to a plea in abatement." (Parsons v. Tickner (1995) 31 Cal.App.4th 1513, 1524.) Plaintiffs, however, are advised to file the necessary affidavit. As to the third cause of action, while lack of informed consent claim sounds in negligence, CACI 533 lists the essential elements of such a claim separate from a claim for medical negligence and thus it is not improper to plead it as a separate cause of action. Moreover, it cannot be determined at this point whether the claim is improperly asserted against the moving defendants. Paragraph 7 of the complaint broadly defines "facility defendants" as including physicians and paragraph 17 alleges that the physician defendants were the employees of the facility defendants. As to the fourth cause of action, if they can do so in good faith, plaintiffs are given leave to allege facts showing their contemporaneous awareness of the injury producing event, e.g., defendants' alleged failure to respond significantly to symptoms that obviously required immediate medical attention. (Keys v. Alta Bates Summit Medical Center (2015) 235 Cal.App.4th 484; Ochoa v. Super. Ct. (1985), 39 Cal.3d 159.). Order signed.
